entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Founder of Careerspan, having a peer conversation with Alex about traction and candidate behavior

entities.name: Alex
entities.context: Peer/advisor with high-trust relationship to V, discussing Careerspan strategy and job-seeker psychology

entities.name: Kevin
entities.context: Careerspan candidate who arrived via job application funnel and immediately recorded multiple stories

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company with ~12 live roles, testing whether candidates will record stories to apply

entities.name: PostHog
entities.context: Analytics tool V uses to track candidate behavior like story starts and completions

entities.name: Intrinsic agency
entities.context: Framework V uses to evaluate candidates - situations of helplessness should provoke approach changes; failing to change is a red flag

entities.name: Blind apply behavior
entities.context: Default job-seeking pattern where candidates mass-apply without extra effort; contrasted with Careerspan's story-based approach

entities.name: Networking anxiety
entities.context: Barrier V personally overcame when company survival forced him to meet people; became effortless through repetition

entities.name: Story recording
entities.context: Careerspan's mechanism for candidate applications; ~15-20% of candidates willing to do this extra work

entities.name: SantaCon
entities.context: San Francisco event mentioned in small talk that set humorous tone for conversation

relationships.context: High-trust peer relationship; strategic conversation about Careerspan traction
V is the founder running Careerspan
Using PostHog to track candidate behavior and story completion rates
Core framework for evaluating candidate quality based on response to helplessness
Personal experience overcoming this when forced by company survival needs
Candidate who used job application funnel and recorded multiple stories
Core mechanism for candidate applications, testing willingness to do extra work
